svg.text: ZULU
HISTORY & NGUNI PEOPLES
NGUNI BANTU ORIGINS
Nguni: branch of Bantu languages spoken along southeastern coast
Zulu, Xhosa, Swati, Ndebele: all Nguni, all share click consonants
Clicks absorbed from Khoisan hunters during southward Bantu migration
Migration complete by ~1000 CE; settled KwaZulu-Natal coast
THE ZULU KINGDOM
Shaka kaSenzangakhona (~1787-1828): founded unified Zulu Kingdom
Shaka reorganized military: ibutho regiment system, iklwa short spear
Mfecane (~1815-1840): Zulu expansion caused massive regional displacement
Battle of Isandlwana (1879): Zulu defeated British regiment — famous victory
ZULU TODAY
~13M speakers; largest ethnolinguistic group in South Africa
KwaZulu-Natal province; Durban largest Zulu-speaking city
Official language of South Africa; Nelson Mandela spoke Zulu
XHOSA HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
XHOSA AND THE CAPE
Xhosa settled Eastern Cape by ~1000-1300 CE; border: Great Fish River
First Africans encountered by Dutch settlers (1652) in significant numbers
9 Cape Frontier Wars (1779-1879): Xhosa vs British/Dutch colonists
Longest colonial resistance in African history
THE GREAT CATTLE KILLING (1856-1857)
Nongqawuse: young Xhosa prophetess; saw vision from ancestors
Prophecy: kill all cattle and destroy crops; ancestors would rise
~400,000 cattle killed; ~40,000 Xhosa died of famine
Catastrophic; weakened Xhosa resistance to colonial annexation
XHOSA AND SOUTH AFRICAN LIBERATION
Nelson Mandela: Thembu/Xhosa; born Mvezo, Eastern Cape
Walter Sisulu, Oliver Tambo: Xhosa ANC leaders
Steve Biko: Xhosa; founder Black Consciousness Movement
~8M speakers; Eastern Cape and Western Cape provinces
ZULU + XHOSA: NGUNI SIBLINGS
Neighboring peoples; mutual intelligibility; shared click heritage
Different colonial histories shaped distinct identities
Both: cornerstone languages of democratic South Africa (11 official)
PLATE 37d ALT · ZULU / XHOSA · HISTORY
